headline: Bumiputera Contractor Class F Licences: 340,000 Issued, 12% Active
context: CIDB data shows 340,000 Class F (small contractor) licences have been issued under Bumiputera reservation, but only 12% are active, suggesting the programme creates licence-holders, not businesses.

cards.big: 340,000 Bumiputera Class F contractor licences exist. Only 12% are active.
cards.sub: The remaining 88% are dormant — many rented to non-Bumiputera contractors for a monthly fee.

cards.big: Class F licence rental generates an estimated RM180M annually.
cards.sub: Licence holders earn RM500-2,000 monthly for lending their Bumiputera status without doing any work.

cards.big: MACC investigated 420 Class F rental cases in 2024.
cards.sub: 12 prosecutions resulted. The practice is too widespread for case-by-case enforcement.

cards.big: 88% dormancy means the programme created 299,000 non-businesses.
cards.sub: By any measure, this is a programme that has failed its stated objective.

cards.big: A programme that creates 340,000 licences and 40,800 businesses is not developing contractors. It is generating rent.

Class F reform should tie licences to demonstrated activity — revenue thresholds, project completion, training certification. A licence without activity is a rent-seeking instrument, not an economic development tool.
